ocr.text: The following table presents an analysis of the impact of various auditory stimuli on the recovery of reproductive toxicity in the
context of nanohydroxyapatite (nHA) exposure. Specifically, it delineates the relationships between the concentration of key
elements, such as Calcium (Ca) and Phosphorus (P), and the observed physiological responses.
Substrates  Atomic percent (%)
C  O  Ca  P  N  S
QSAR models  Positive chemicals (n)  Positive chemicals (%)
Reproductive toxicity  5240
Teratogenic risk or rodent dominant lethal or Drosophila m. SLRL
Reproductive toxicity and androgen receptor antagonism  278  5.3
Reproductive toxicity and estrogen reporter gene  172  3.3
Reproductive toxicity and estrogen α receptor binding  244  4.7
Index  Control  Recovery 1  Baroque music  Recovery 2  Heavy metal music  p
The metrics of interest include the number of positive chemicals (n) identified in the study, which stands at 5,240, and the
corresponding percentage of these chemicals, calculated at 66.85%. The incorporation of QSAR models further enhances our
understanding of the chemical interactions at play. Additionally, the table illustrates the differential effects of various musical
stimuli, such as Baroque music and Heavy metal music, on recovery metrics following the exposure. These findings underscore
the significance of auditory environments in modulating chemical toxicity and recovery, offering a nuanced perspective on the
interplay between environmental factors and biochemical responses. Such insights are crucial for developing more effective
strategies in toxicology and environmental health.
